Details:
Results of one study suggested that the presence of hydrogen peroxide increased the removal of benzene when irradiated with ultraviolet light in comparison to UV treatment alone as well as UV treatment with the addition of TiO2 (TiO2 data is presented under the Benzene/UV Irradiation pairing). This was the case for a synthetic aqueous solution, as well as contaminated groundwater in both batch and flow-through experiments. The addition of 130 or 2000 mg/L of hydrogen peroxide increased benzene removal to between 94 to greater than 99 percent. This is likely due to the increased formation of hydroxyl radicals which are a strong oxidizer.
April 2009
